rules.title: Provide downloadable patch scripts
rules.description: Generated repository patch work is delivered as a downloadable shell script artifact.
rules.rationale: The user runs patches through the local download runner and needs a stable file artifact.
checks: final answer links the generated artifact

rules.title: Patch scripts include repodossier metadata
rules.description: Every patch script starts with a patch metadata record and progress records.
rules.rationale: The c runner validates metadata before executing a patch.
checks: patch record has id, title and commit

rules.title: Use anchors for future progress metadata
rules.description: Progress metadata can reference stable anchors instead of fragile line numbers.
rules.rationale: Anchors survive ordinary roadmap and milestone edits better than raw line ranges.
checks: anchor occurs in the referenced file
start and end remain accepted for compatibility
start and end are provided together when used

rules.title: Avoid deprecated bundle workflow
rules.description: Patch scripts do not call the old bundle_project workflow.
rules.rationale: RepoDossier exports now replace the old bundle workflow.
checks: documentation points to repodossier exports instead

rules.title: Do not install global logging or clipboard hooks
rules.description: Patch scripts do not use global tee redirection or clipboard tools.
rules.rationale: The c runner owns logging and terminal failure handling.

rules.title: Avoid pager-based git output
rules.description: Patch scripts use no-pager git output for diffs and logs.
rules.rationale: Pager output can block copy-pasted terminal runs.

rules.title: Run focused tests before commit
rules.description: Patch scripts run relevant pytest or syntax checks before committing.
rules.rationale: Commits should only be created for green focused validation.
checks: syntax checks run for changed scripts
focused tests run for changed behavior
commit is skipped or aborted when tests fail

rules.title: Commit only staged successful changes
rules.description: Patch scripts stage intended files and commit only when staged changes exist.
rules.rationale: No empty commits and no accidental unrelated changes.
checks: git add names explicit changed paths
commit message matches the requested commit target

rules.title: Print a color-coded progress footer
rules.description: Patch scripts end with a green, purple and yellow progress summary.
rules.rationale: The user can see completed, current and next work at a glance.
checks: footer contains next work where applicable
red is reserved for actual problems

rules.title: c dry-run does not mutate scripts
rules.description: The c dry-run path validates but does not execute or move the patch script.
rules.rationale: Dry-run is a safety check and must not change download state.
checks: dry-run does not execute patch body
dry-run does not move to done
dry-run does not move to failed
dry-run prints DRY-RUN OK when successful

rules.title: c does not leak internal runner environment
rules.description: Patch scripts do not inherit internal C_RUNNER variables.
rules.rationale: Tests run nested c subprocesses and must not behave like wait children.

rules.title: Document workflow changes in patch rules
rules.description: New patch-runner behavior is recorded in the human-readable rules document.
rules.rationale: The rules file remains the operator-facing source of truth.
checks: machine-readable rules are referenced from patch-rules.md
